suggested_title: Julianna Cruz - MD/PhD Student at NYU Grossman School of Medicine
prospect_notes: First-generation, Mexican American student who graduated from Emory University in May 2025 with a B.S. in Anthropology & Human Biology and a B.A. in Spanish/Portuguese. 2024 Goldwater Scholar who conducted kidney disease and nephrology research in Dr. Jeanie Park's renal research lab and completed the SUPERR (Summer Undergraduate Program in Emory Renal Research) fellowship. Presented her research at the World Congress of Nephrology in Buenos Aires, Argentina, representing Emory's commitment to international scientific collaboration. Plans to pursue an MD/PhD program, committed to NYU Grossman School of Medicine. Strong interest in bridging basic science and clinical research in renal medicine to improve outcomes for underserved patient populations.

suggested_title: Sabrina Li - MD Student at Stanford University School of Medicine
prospect_notes: Biology major who graduated from Emory University in May 2025 and enrolled at Stanford University School of Medicine beginning fall 2025. Conducted undergraduate research in Dr. Jeanie Park's renal research lab at Emory and completed the SUPERR (Summer Undergraduate Program in Emory Renal Research) fellowship alongside classmate Julianna Cruz. Also founded Emory's Slow Food chapter as an undergraduate, demonstrating leadership and engagement in food systems and community health. Confirmed enrollment at Stanford School of Medicine noted in an Emory University news profile of her research lab peer.

suggested_title: Ilakkia Anabayan - MD Student at Yale School of Medicine
prospect_notes: Emory University alumna who graduated with a B.S. in Neuroscience and Behavioral Biology (Class of 2022), where she was a 2021 Goldwater Scholar for her research on pediatric traumatic brain injury (TBI) in the Friess Lab at Washington University in St. Louis. Awarded the Churchill Scholarship — Emory's first since 1988 — to pursue a master's degree in clinical neurosciences at the University of Cambridge. Published 4 research papers with 81+ citations on ResearchGate, including work on antibiotic exposure in severe pediatric TBI and neuroprotection mechanisms. Currently enrolled at Yale School of Medicine (expected graduation 2027) and serving as a Solomon Student Fellow at Yale Law School's Solomon Center for Health Law and Policy. Research interests span pediatric neurotrauma, gut microbiome interactions with TBI, and health law/policy.
